title: SACRAMENTO, CA —
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1264 aims to phase out 'particularly harmful' ultra-processed foods (UPFs) from school meals in California by 2032.",
    "State scientists, in cooperation with experts from the University of California, will identify UPFs linked to health issues such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and metabolic disorders.",
    "Ultra-processed foods are engineered for overconsumption and can hijack the brain's reward system, making it difficult for individuals to reduce their intake.",
    "Consumption of ultra-processed foods is associated with rising rates of obesity, diabetes, and diet-related diseases, especially in children.",
    "Poor nutrition in childhood, primarily due to processed foods high in added sugars, contributes to lifelong risks for chronic diseases, including obesity and type 2 diabetes.",
    "Scientific research has linked ultra-processed foods to serious health harms, including cancer, cardiovascular disease, and mental health issues.",
    "A systematic review confirmed the link between consumption of ultra-processed foods and greater risks of being overweight or obese.",
    "73 percent of U.S. adults aged 20 and older are overweight or obese, and 38 percent of children and youth between 12 and 19 years old are prediabetic due to poor dietary choices, including UPFs.",
    "Excessive consumption of ultra-processed foods is a leading driver of poor health outcomes and rising healthcare costs."
  ]
}
```
